content: it's steam on the phone, and that's pretty cool. easy access to your library, friends, steam store, and while you obviously can't play your pc games from your phone, having access to all of this is great. the only thing that would make this app better is if it was made more to fit a mobile atmosphere. while the layout works fine, it's sharp edges make it feel occasionally akward to press things.

glad to see a lot of the full client features have been added to the mobile version. including tags, [purchased, ignored, and wish listed marks when looking at items in the store without having to open the item page], ability to redeam steam monetary cards without having to go to the webpage to redeem [has to be done through account settings]. it has been a while since i updated my review.

so far i like the app except for a couple of problems. 1. i really wish you'd add the option to remove and hide games from your library and not just on the pc version of the app. 2. i wish you'd make it easier to also add the free games to your library instead of having to go to the page for the game and tap "add to library" and then go back to the store page and scroll down to the bottom again just to add the next game every time. if you added those features the app would be great imo.

it's sometimes laggy but. it does everything i need it to. you can buy games, remote download games, add money, log into other devices, etc etc. no ads or anything stupid. to be clear this doesnt let tou play pc games, that's not how this works, it just let's you manage steam stuff. again it's often laggy. good tho

it's gotten a lot better, though i've noticed that you cannot save an image you send to your phone. you'd have to send it to your email, or send it to friends to save the image. despite saying save/share your image.. used to have an option to download, and now you don't.

works pretty decent for its purpose. steam guard works pretty much every time, but it doesn't send a notification for logins without steam guard (like approving a 3rd party app). some specific pages will open in a browser before immediately redirecting back to the app, like the pages for the new hardware, and some random menu buttons when navigating. horizontal carousels have a tendency to be anywhere between sluggish to violently flipping through 20 items in a second.

it works fine for me ... but when did they stop showing what friends own a game on the store page? am i imagining it? i could have sworn i could see who owns a game on mobile. do they still show wishlisted by friends? it's one of the main features i use... mobil steam is mostly useless for me without it.

it's alright, not the perfect companion app, not really great, but it works decently well for browsing and checking in things. people who thought this was like the desktop program are stupid as hell, why and how would that work?
is there a way to view the sale under £7 or £4 much bigger it's kinda hard to see. and what genre of the game is missing as well or isn't showing up.
please add a feature that allows us to view our "saved in steam" clips for games from the new recording system. not just uploaded clips or screenshots.

the steam authenticator issue sucks.. filed a ticket in but no responses still from steam support for 2 days already. i was hoping to play my purchased games during holidays, but it seems like i need to switch to a different app.. just dissapointing.

great app. i don't know why it has a bad rating it pretty much does what the website does except play games but everything else is normal (edit: can you guys add a search bar when going into the workshop and finding mods it's kinda hard a to find specific mods when using the app.)

the steam app has improved significantly over the years. although it sucks that steam chat is it's own entirely different app. either way, browsing the store and checking community posts is pretty streamlined now, barely come across any hassle.

steam on mobile is not a games platform , its a store with connection to your library. there are log in issues and anytime i have a link to steam that goes to the app it sends me to it and then the app store. other than that it holds its own and retains purchases.
cannot use the mobile app. won't let me log in and have no way to get in touch with anyone. steam is great, just mobile app sucks.

i am not giving this 5 stars only because of the rating. it is rated t for teen so that limits certain nsfw images on the mobile app if you play adult games. why not up the rating to m for mature so we don't have any restrictions? other than that it does everything else well and i had no issues logging in.
good app, use it to browse games and buy games when i cant open my computer. but sometimes it stops functioning suddenly
honestly not as bad as people say it is. it can be slow sometimes however some other features like the login make up for it
steam is fine however i wish you can play your pc games however i don't think your device can run them well especially with games like sonic frontiers and elden ring lol
when i try to enter library on steam mobile it doesn't open, says "oops, something went wrong while loading the page." fix this issue
good app, although not 5 star as issues seeing all games in steam library despite leaving family view.

does most things that i need it to, and it's pretty useful for browsing wherever you are. the low review score honestly confuses me. there are a couple of things that it could do with, though. navigating to redeem steam keys is unnecessarily complicated on the app, so it'd be great to see a menu option to access it easily instead of having to go to a specific support page and clicking a link. it could also do with the option to message people. that shouldn't need a separate app. that'd be great
it's alright, but please, add the option to undo the "like button" on the workshop
good mobile version but its lacks in smooth animations
i don't know if you guys gonna see this review or not but if you do can you bring chat back in the app instead of a separate app
hey steam you can give one other option for payment like upi it is a good app to buy games i also buy different types of games
could use a bit of organizing for the app itself but its a good way to authorize you logins
they should add a feature or downloading soundtracks you've bought

nice for the authenticator and chatting with friends, but the store ui can be clunky as all hell, especially during event sales where the special event page (eg. the christmas sale advent calendar) is just copy-pasted from the desktop version, which doesn't exactly make it mobile-friendly. other than that, perfectly serviceable and usable.

it's a pretty good app. yeah it's sometimes laggy as it's just a webview but it works. two things would be nice. if we could go to a game's workshop page straight from a game's page in the library, and if we have media that's been uploaded from a steam deck, if we could just save the file directly and not be forced to share it.

works as an authentication app, not much else. store regularly doesn't load, the library is too zoomed in to be navigable, and the profile is too zoomed out to be helpful. this is fine, nobody is trying to replace the website/desktop app with this, but lord help you if you do. but the authenticator works perfectly, and that's all you should care about.

incoming notifications that "the game from wishlist is on sale right now " won't tell you which particular game it is.. i understand that it would be difficult to fit all titles in one notification if it's a big sale when large amount of games set sale simultaneously, but on the other side, i receive these notifications every day, and it's not informative while i have >200 titles in wishlist. so i have to check email... anyway imho, notifications has a potential for upgrade.

it works alright and it's nice to view something on steam quickly without my computer. it's a little buggy (some buttons don't register as being clicked on) and the ui is very different than what's on the computer so i get lost a lot.
the bad reviews are genuinely botted this app works fine the only bad thing is the ui which idgaf if i find the games i wanne play

i mean i'm writing this to write a review about the company and not the app the app is low-key a little slow. but anyhow i got phished and due to circumstances could not reply to the steam emails about account recovery but to my disbelief over like 3-4 weeks later i could still retrieve my account and they replied in under 8 hours too!! very good customer support!

i love using this app to browse for fun games and buy them. however, there are two big-ish issues i keep encountering: when buying a game it takes too long for the app to understand that i have paid and the page 'thank you for your purchase' gets shown, making it a bit stressful to be sure i actually got the game i paid for. secondly, i kinda hate that when i'm exploring a sideswipe list, tap a game then press 'back', it takes me to the top of the last page, instead of where i was in the list

not had any issues since 08/11/25. *update* steam on mobile is a little drunk when using you discovery queue. the "next in queue" button has vanished and i have to rotate my phone sideways for it to appear. and the game trailers and videos refuse to play longer than 5 seconds of footage before skipping to the still images.

such a good app. i have one small suggestion, if possible, it would be great if we could queue remote downloads while the pc is off, to start when the pc next connects to steam. if it can be done, it would be a great quality of life feature.

each item on the wishlist now has a large image, making the entries far too large and the wishlist quite unmanageable for me. the compact view on the other hand provides far too little information... please offer me a compromise or let me opt out of seeing the images. and please bring back "early access" indicator for titles on wishlist, thx!

this app allows you to see your steam profile and the steam store, which means you can buy games remotely. it also contains steam guard which is a nice added layer of protection for your account. the only problem is everything takes a while to load or load in.

app is good, i just wish it would have a setting where you can type your pc specs an it would pop up all the games that your pc can run at medium to high settings because some of these games be lying about the specs. please add that feature.

a few months ago, this app looked like trash, but now it looks like something i want to actually use. there are a few issues regarding loading the wishlist and some other minor instabilities. however, they don't make me miss the old app. keep up the good work! update: i wish there was a tab for the library page in the bottom toolbar. something like the deck ui where i can schedule an install or just check achievements or dlc and the description. the current ui with all the dropdowns is tedious.
good but i wish they put steam and steam chat into one app, i don't understand why messaging is separate
but better of you add chat and i can talk with friends in the same app

good experience overall really like being able to surf the catalog with ease. notifications for when items on your wishlist are on sale is good to lose 10 minutes in. but it would be nice if it also notified you on pre orders that you need to make payments for, for example a valve index kit on backorder (i know theres an email sent to you but i think it would be a nice feature).

i have spend years on steam on many accounts, the best customer service in the game with many deals and sales on games. only problem i have is when listing a item on the desktop version it asks for a confirmation from the steam app which just gets annoying after a time, beside that well made app with a well working company

the app is great for browsing games and for account security but can you fix the issue with gcash? i've been using the app to purchase games but it really limits me every time i away from my pc to buy games. the app is convenient but restricting in some areas.

bruh, i haven't bought or tried to buy anything before i attempted to purchase cyberpunk. it's telling me i have to wait an hour before i can buy anything now... anyways, there's a bug with this where i can't edit my card details when this happens. you can delete your card details, but you won't be able to add it back. this seems like a waste of time to me.

i recently tried to purchased a game on steam using my debit card. however, the transaction failed, but the money was still debited from my account. as a new user, i turned to steam support for help. instead of resolving my issue, they directed me to another email. after emailing them, i received no response. this has been an extremely frustrating experience, and i'm very disappointed with the lack of proper support from steam. i expected much better service from such a well known platform.

paypal not working? used to log in automatically. now it wants me to login again, except putting an email in and hitting "next" just clears the email every time. card and the rest if the app still works, but it's less convenient. may just be me though. will add that like other reviews say, it's clunky, but i only use it to check sales and buy games so idk about managing library or workshop etc.

it is a very good app, but compared to the pc version it could be better. notifications don't always come through, if you have a private account, sometimes it can refuse to load your inventory. a feature i'd like to see id the ability to see whether a person has read your message(s). other than that it is decent.

why can't i add stickers on gifts? 😢 can i see who watch my broadcast? when uninstalling steam, it could have an option to keep game files, to avoid re-downloading (on a reinstall) .. some games need admin rights... are soundtracks available on the mobile app? prealocating before discovering existing files of a game, needs the double space!? ws colection all user update, only-on-launch does not work... steam should be able to download videos for offline, no lagging, loading & better resolution!

the app is quite useful for buying games, browsing the steam store, reviewing and the like. the only downside is the fact that you can't send dms to your friends through the app and it redirects to the browser. you can also use the community market for all the levelling freaks out there, so get your badges in order. all in all, would recommend purely because you can snag great deals from your phone in case you don't have access to a computer.

excellent way to purchase games and chat with friends. also good for viewing community posts. my only gripe is that i wish you could stream soundtracks of games (that you purchased the soundtrack of, of course) through the app. you can pheasably do everything else you can do on steam through the app (except play an unsupported game, but that can't be faulted) and it's easy to use. also useful is the steam guard, which helps protect your steam account.

the app itself is brilliant, don't really have anything against it, but there is just one thing. if you have a game on your wishlist and it goes on sale... it automatically logos you in so you're ready to buy it. kind of annoying. but you'll get over it...

very useful for keeping up with chats with friends. plus steam guard for security. i would give 5, but it's a 4 for one reason only. there's no way to see servers you've added to your favourites. it would be nice to see if people are playing & so on. so please steam, add that option next 😀

i like it, but....... the new ui looks great, or at least comparing to the older version. although i can't understand why you would remove the chat section, it is after all the point of downloading the steam mobile app or at least for me, staying connected should be kept in mind.

pretty good for account security, with the two factor authentification. works great with the desktop version. doing anything else can be a little clunky, like trying to view a profile. its nice to message steam friends here if they dont have discord. trade and other confimations are very glitched right now. if you click tes its going to look like it ignored it and get screwy but it actually goes through, so dont worry.

the app, has no bugs on my experience. and the app. is secure because of dual authenticator. the app has did it's purpose so that you can see and buy games on pocket edition it saves time and hustle because you don't really needed to power on ur pc always just to buy and scroll games on pc which is time consuming. my suggestion is when in library have option on the games to see your bought dlc's of the game, it's name and picture or feature i guese this maybe you know how many dlc's you have.

i updated the app and it's way better than it was, but still just ok. i love the new look, but wish you could toggle to a list of game names instead of giant photos. gripes below: you can't scroll by flicking your finger, you have to hold your finger down the entire length of your screen. i try to go to the marketplace for trading cards and it gives an error saying not able to connect, no matter the game. it keeps asking for access to my camera, even though i'm not trying to use the qr code.

all in all, the app works way better than it did before. most bugs i personally ran into have been fixed, which is good. the only reason i'm giving it a 4 star instead of 5 is for the reason that they made the decision to keep the steam chat as a separate app, which, in my opinion, is annoying. the chat function used to be in this app in the older version, and i'm still not sure why they decided it was a better idea to remove it and make a separate app for it. please bring back the chat function

it is great to use, and i can get games and dlcs before i get home. can be a bit hard to navigate sometimes, but it's something to get used to. i do have one slight issue though which is the fact that after installing a dlc for ets2 the app still said it was "in cart" and only when i tap on it says "in library" this can get very confusing but since i got an email saying said dlc had been purchased i knew i had it.

you can verify your account with a code even in offline mode without using internet which is good for those with a cheaper data plan and valve app are almost perfect despite there now being two apps. one for steam guard and another for chat. points taken for the procedure taking longer with more clicks though but all in all good overall.

the steam store, but on your phone. no, this doesn't let you play steam games, this is just the store itself. there are some things wrong with it, like sometimes you can't go back unless you press menu > your store > home. i wouldn't suggest using it if youre writing a guide or a long post, just use the pc version instead. i'd give it 3 and a half stars if i could.

please add a way to download demos remotely that haven't been installed yet. i added some demos to my library and i wanted to download them remotely to the steam deck from the app, but i noticed they don't show up in my library. apparently, they have to be installed at least once for them to show up in the library on mobile? seems like a weird oversight and it kind of takes away value from using the mobile app in the first place.

good app to check steam community market although the trading restrictions for any item are a bit annoying to take off by giving phone number but oh well. also good games on here. (problem with the chat whenever a friend is typing the chat goes up and down because the three little dots [ ... ] are put in the chat as well, so when they stop typing it essentially deletes a message making the chat go all wonky.)

running crisp in 2023 which is a must anymore. i am without a pc atm and dont have the funding to access my 2k worth of content. this companion app helps me ensure my games are secure. a great companion app overall. they just need to make "profile" "store" "library" "community" and "companion" (which includes your 2factor auth. and security settings) the main tabs on the bottom of the screen for ease of accessibility imo. also i have 0 ads and hope it is kept that way. not worth the headache.

wish it was desktop ¯\_(ツ)_/¯ steam mobile is good for browsing the store, chatting with friends, and little else. if it has more features than that, they aren't easily accessible. i really wish i could download my music, which is the reason i downloaded steam mobile at all. that said, what it can do it does well, hence the 4/5 since while it's disappointing, expecting the app to be tailor-made for my experience would be dumb lol

i don't use the steam app for much, mainly 2fa for my steam account. i do like the new qr scan method, hopefully it's as secure as a separate authenticator device. i do wish that you could somehow access soundtracks you've bought and either play them or just make the audio files available for other apps. probably won't happen (licensing and increased app complexity), but a guy can dream.

the app is much better now, easier to find things and a little less buggy. they still need to modify the app's performance, it's still a little stuck but it's very good for me. also need do something with the steam link, and continue with it, i think it's a great idea. and the steam chat could be in the same app :) i think it's much better now, for me just some things take a while to load, i think people are being very critical of the app

as a steam user this for me work's just great.haven't found anything wrong with it yet ?. it sign's on automatically once's you've put in you're password, and from there you can review your account details , your game's and what's for sale or the latest release or just look up facts or information in a given genre's, that you are interested in. you can also purchase a game or games that you like, you will have to upload your bank account details to that section of the app for it to work.😎

i uninstalled this for awhile due to some issues, but now it's getting better! but... there are some problems between syncing from my computer to my mobile steam. example, i have 24 screenshots, but when i look at the app, it says i only have 8. push notifications don't really work when i'm messaging friends. if those could be fixed, i'd be content. 2020 edit: much better! when i'm away from home during a sale, i no longer have to fight with this app to buy games or message friends!

update: card payment seems to work again for me -------–----- very good for browsing the store, ironically, it appears that the card protection redirect process breaks the process as it's handled in a seperate browser app, and so steam isn't taking my money. when i see a game i really like and go "steam, take my money!", they should be able to take it, but at present i have to use the store on pc or mobile browser to set up a cart instead.

the application works well for me but it's been frustrating for some of my friends with newer galaxy phones. they add games to their libraries however the game does not appear there. when they goto the game it states that it has been added to the library. once in awhile i may have to close the app out completely then start it again to update/refresh but that does nothing to help my galaxy friends. hopefully it'll be resolved soon.

has its bugs and issues, but as a mobile app, works what it should be doing, allowing you to confirm trades or market listings and also adding a bit of security to it, definitely needed though as if you want to trade, you will have to wait more than 2 weeks to make a trade, which is a big waste of time.

i love steam, and they need an overhaul. given they were the first one to provide all devs a platform for game management with users from transaction to install to support, i still have a soft spot for them (and quite the extensive library), but these times they are a changing... steam app needs basics 1) redo the ux for ease of use -- slim it down some 2) sharing -- include email + copy + txt msg in the app (everyone does this now) 3) video playback is intermittent even on wifi

the good: i like the new streamlined search options. nice job guys! the bad: i have to constantly re-login into the app and use steam guard at the same time. found a bug where the profile button is broken, where it takes me back to the last game i looked at. please give us the option to sort wishlist on mobile! i use it to track prices as well as unreleased games.

it works for the most part. appears to be a loading loop bug with the library though. about half the time when you select a game nothing loads unless you restart the app. update nov 24. some new bugs lately, the library is missing some games and statistics (playtime, achievements) say zero on some games. i believe this started when the new family share beta was released. still 4 stars does what i need with a few bugs.

payment bug prevents me from giving this 5 stars. use it mostly to buy/browse sales when i'm away. occasionally it'll re-prompt a full paypal login. i get why they do it, but i'd type my paypal number or email and it refuses to go beyond that. even missed out on a sale cause i was told i made too many purchases when i couldn't even process the sale. so i'd have to get on a desktop to verify my login again. other than that it serves its purpose well in everything else as an app.

great platform just some things that make it less than ideal, when you go to download things onto your selected device it ignores whatever is in progress and starts the download of the last thing selected which can make it feel like downloads take forever. i'd much rather have the option to "lock" downloads so what i want downloaded first doesn't get booted when i decide i also want another game for later.

updated review: previously i had left a very negative review due to the reworks made in 2022. since then, i've adjusted to them and no longer have massive problems like i did then. but, there is still a few issues i have. adding games to your wishlist is difficult since you need to go to the games store page instead of tapping and holding for the pop-up. the app also constantly resets to the homepage after having it closed for just a little bit. other than that, it's much better than it was.

the sign-in process was a bit confusing, but overall it's a decent app. i might seem dumb, but i was hoping for it to allow play of games on mobile, and i am not that disappointed seeing that it can not. if you want to play steam games you'll still need a computer, this app is just useful for using steam freely without the constraint of your computer.

new login authentication is a bit different but nothing i can't get used to. other than that the app seems kinda laggy when sitting on the qr scanner page. also, was never a fan of qr codes and probably won't be in the future, so it would be preferable if there was a steam guard landing page that wasn't just the qr code scanner.

the video preview panel doesn't support older versions of android, which is poor considering its just a media player. other than that, its a good app, which gives you full control of your steam library, and a way to browse steam sales, etc, when you're away from your pc.

it's pretty handy! lets me keep in contact with my steam friends on the go. store can be a bit buggy, so i usually don't use it. also, i don't think i can access group chats, which is a little annoying. regardless, the steam app has become one i download on every phone i get.

the steam app has come a long way. the newest update looks cleaner, seems more functional, and qr code & confirmation logins are my favorite additions to using steam. epic, origins, and uplay launchers are very behind. that's not to say steam did began with a terrible launcher but they learned. steam has more experience building a game launcher than any other publisher game launcher out there. the steam launcher is my favorite place to buy games and sell & trade vanity items.

sometimes transactions won't go through if you change the default payment, which uses steam wallet alongside visa/default. could improve with a tick box that says "use steam wallet". sometimes the app bugs out, making the return button exit the app, instead of return to home page/previous page. being able to sign into steam on different machines and browsers with the badge icon and qr is great. able to change the 5 icons is great. i have one for my market items because of my backlog.

i really do love having this app on my phone. i can pull up my account and show others on the spot or look up games and such but my only problem is despite all the updates, my wishlist never loads on the app. i've left it open for almost an hour before trying to let it load and it never did. i really wish it'd get looked into.

it's okay ... for now. it seems every couple months, it will break one way or another, where something within the app is broken or not working properly. but right now, on my pixel 7 pro, it's working correctly and i can't complain. good way to catch sales when away from you pc.

giving it a 4 as no option for a 3.5 or so. i never tend to use it for more than 2fa, but was pleased to find remote install of existing library or mobile purchases an option. being able to see full install size, and assign alternate library location instead of default folder if you have multiple set up would be nice. store and purchase went smooth enough. can be a bit clunky and slow, but good enough to grab a flash sale on the go before it ends.

slight delay compared to the pc, other than that it works great. could use a few tweaks as mentioned above...news is sometimes has delayed. steam support needs an option to simply contact support without reason, and any free game or demo should be able to be added to your library without the client you can't run on your phone. but thats all minor (news delay aside), its useful to purchase games on the go and keep up with subscribed posts and friends. overall, great app.

if you're expecting to play games, don't. you won't be able to, hence the low score. otherwise this app works completely fine for buying stuff, viewing videos, reviews, normal stuff you could do in any regular website. steam support remains absolute #1 in my opinion, got my account back after giving details, refunds less than a day, even answers to questions about games i bought. videos are rather laggy but that could be my device. if you could play games on mobile, that'd make this app #1

it's slow but it works, most of the time, and doesn't collect excessive personal info and does not sell it to 3rd parties. the desktop app is what i take more issue with, as several months ago, it went from an already slowish 5 seconds on my fast gaming laptop to start, to 20 seconds, all because of a useless splash screen!

authenticator keeps my account safe, sure. but to make any changes or interact with anything within the steam market you have to go through the confirmation system in the app... it doesnt work!!! just spams you with confirmation errors. valve, fix this and stop timeouting me and everyone else trying to send confirmations to our account emails because your app doesnt work.

the app is fantastic for purchasing games and viewing more info about games you're interested in, the app itself has some flaws where you hit the back button and it often just minimizes the app, or when you're watching game trailers or videos the control/timeline bar does not fade away and blocks a bit of the screen while watching videos on the app. i've also had a few problems with purchasing, either my info won't be remembered, or information won't load to make the purchase i want. good app.

exactly what you need as a handheld version of steam, for messaging, managing inventory, game management etc... only thing i would say is that the menus can be a little difficult to navigate due to their names, it's something i got used to but a friend being a new user found it a little counter intuitive.

(note: this review is for the pc steam. i didn't know how to review pc steam.) great! so many games to choose from and a great community! but just one problem, i would really like it if you added a privacy settings feature so i can hide my games from other people. i know there is already a feature for this, but it also hides lots of other things for your profile and it basically hides almost your whole profile. i would like it if i could hide my games individually. reply if more info needed.

it's good for keeping an eye on my account or checking out new games/updates. that said, the discussion forums for games are a mess. they are almost impossible to navigate and make sense of. forums are not optimized for a phone. i really hope this is something they fix as it has been an issue for a long time. i frequently check forums on steam but if i am away from home and need to use my phone, i go to the steam website instead, which seems redundant when having the app.

would be a great app if not for some obvious annoyances. steam guard is great, but for most other things the performance is sluggish. there's no easy way of resetting the authenticator if you log out of both your pc and phone. generally valve forcing you to use this app if you want to trade in a meaningful way. again normal users having to suffer because a few idiots got scammed.

it works with all of the basic features you'd want with a steam mobile app. it also includes a simple authentication program. besides that it's missing some nice features i feel it should have. it should let you download games to a signed in and currently running steam app on a computer and then allow you to even pick the drive it is on. another useful feature would be the ability to subscribe to and remove/edit workshop content from the app for any game on the account that uses workshop.

4 stars because as solid an app as it is even when i'm using it on my foldable (which app developers seem to never consider) i still think steam chat should be integrated rather than a separate app. i can understand it for steam link but for chat it feels nonsensical and any convenience had from being able to access steam from my phone is somewhat lost keeping chat feature on a different app.

this app is great but it's too secure, i've changed phones twice now and the issue is the sim i have has had a different number put on it, my original number that is. and since then i haven't been able to gain access to steam guard on any other device. i've tried deactivating and re activating but it still says the number is wrong and denies me access.
add the hide games feature and i will give 5 star

downloaded it just a few minutes ago and logged in just okay. not 100% satisfied with it because it does not load my wishlist for some reason. it just says "an error was encountered while processing your request." my wishlist flashed for a split second before i got the error message.😔

it's fine. but seriously, the softkey back button on the phone shouldn't close the app. it should work as a page back button like in an internet browser...it's such a pain to have to go back to store homepage and swipe back down where i once was to continue browsing. it makes the browsing experience terrible.

app is generally okay. my only major problem is when you search for games etc, the results (including logos) are misalligned and very hard to view the title. also if you select an item to view it in detail but then want to go back to the results, it takes you back too far and u have to search all over again

i primarily use the app for perusing my wishlist, often due to notifications. overall it does this sufficiently. however, sometimes the android back button/gesture doesn't take you back to the wishlist as expected, it instead takes you back to the store (this is when getting to the wishlist from the store page). update: found the option to have a wishlist tab and it keeps filters and order. so i've updated my review and increased to 4 stars.

its great and all, but feels like of they could improve the ui a bit theyd earn way more money because then people will browse the store from their phones a bit more when they are bored or travelling and buy more games. currently, browsing the store on this is terrible. in the end, most of the times i use it is for steam guard.

hey guys - for steam, this is a store browser and account manager (+ more). this is not a game launcher. steam on your pc/linux/apple machine is a launcher (license distributor/verifier). the games you want to play are designed by the developers to run on those operating systems previously mentioned above. it's okay if you didn't realize prior to reading this review. no where in the description for this app does it say "launcher" "play games". use steam link to stream games from client.

still getting the hang of it... mostly what i would like to see is a small memory foot print and cpu utilization whilst running in the background. i've used application for approximately six months now. due to the nature of it, i'm reserving further review here to give it a fair shake out.
